title: clematis cirrhosa f. semitriloba
intro: Clematis cirrhosa f. semitriloba, also known as the evergreen clematis, is a type of flowering vine that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clematis cirrhosa f. semitriloba are said to include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its roots and flowers, and can be found in the form of teas, infusions, or dried extracts.
